description: Introduction Kudremukh National Park perched at an altitude of 1894 m. above the sea level in Chikmangalur in Karnataka is famous for some very good reasons. Named after a hill top that is shaped like the head of a horse, this national park spreads across 600 sq.km. Tucked deep into the Western Ghats, Kudremukh National Park is made up of four different ranges of Kudremukh, Kerekatte, Kalasa, and Shimoga. The verdant valleys and the lush green tea and coffee plantations around give the park an ethereal beauty sans parallel. The grassy landscape dotted with patches of thickly wooded forests offer an enchanting sight to the visitors. The park is home to a diverse flora and fauna and is categorized under the Global Tiger Conservation Priority-I, as per the format developed by the Wildlife Conservation Society (WCS) and World Wide Fund-USA. It is home to an astonishing range of wildlife including tigers, Malabar giant squirrels, langurs, porcupines, wild dogs and gaurs among others, some of which are highly endangered. Kudremukh National Park also holds the distinction of being the origin of three rivers the Nethravathi, the Tunga and the Bhadra. The ancient cave temples of Varaha and Bhagavati attract scores of pilgrims from far and wide. Adventure lovers would find trekking to the Kudremukh peak an exciting activity. Trekkers are allowed from 6:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.Hanuman Gundi Waterfalls (open from 10:00 am to 4:00 pm) is another essential stopover here. Guided walks are the best possible options to explore the park and the surroundings.

description: An important national park of the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve, Nagarhole is also a popular Tiger Reserve. Covered with dense forests and home to many spectacular streams and waterfalls, the park is well-known for impressive numbers of tigers, elephants and Indian bison. Once a hunting reserve of the Mysore rulers, it is today one of the most visited wildlife reserves in the South India. Leopard, wild dog, sloth bear, jackal, striped hyena, sambar, chital, four-horned antelope, civets and flying squirrels are among other popular wild animals at Nagarhole. The park is also recognized as an important bird area with species like vultures, lesser adjutant, wood-pigeons, eagles, darters, oriental ibis, parakeets and hornbills commonly spotted. Safari trips are organized twice a day in jeeps and elephant rides are also available. You need to pay additional fees for safari and guides. The park roads are strictly closed for traffic from 06:00 p.m. to 06:00 a.m.

description: The second largest palace in Mysore, Lalitha Mahal lies very close to the Chamundi Hills. It is built in the lines of St. Paul's Cathedral in London. Originally built by the Maharaja of Mysore, Krishnaraja Wodeyar IV as an exclusive residence for Viceroy of India, it was later converted into a royal guesthouse where the king used to entertain European guests. The architectural style of this imposing structure is a subtle blend of that of an Italian palazzo and an English manor house highlighted by the ionic double columns, extended porch and the spherical domes. The interiors of this double storied palace are regal with stained glass windows, ceilings and doors. Belgian crystal chandeliers and the Italian marble floors and staircases add up to the elegance of its sprawling interiors. The dance room, banquet hall and the viceroy's room of the palace still retain their original glory. The palace offers great views of the Chamundi Hills and the Mysore city. The palace was converted into a 5-star heritage hotel in 1974 by Indian Tourism Development Corporation (ITDC) and has been used as the backdrop for many Indian movies.

description: A perfect example of Indian architectural beauty, Mysore Palace resembles the Mughal and French culture through its design and decor. The magnificent building, Mysore Palace is also popular as Amba Vilas Palace. Enhancing the value, beauty and image of Mysore, this palace is located in the heart of the city and introduces tourists to the grandeur of a bygone era. An ideal amalgamation of Oriental, Roman, Indo-Saracenic and Dravidian architectural styles, Mysore Palace spells magic. Now the most renowned and most visited tourist attraction in India, Mysore Palace has two durbar halls that are embellished with beautiful gardens and courtyards. The palace is lighted from 07:00 p.m. to 7:45 p.m. which enhances its beauty further. The palace has three unique rooms – Ambavilasa (one of the most beautiful rooms), Gombe Thotti (a gallery of traditional dolls) and Kalyana Mantapa (the marriage hall). Mysore Palace complex houses twelve spectacular Hindu temples like Someshvara Temple, Shwetha Varahaswamy Temple and Lakshmiramana Temple etc. Other attractions in Mysore include Lalitha Mahal Palace, the Railway Museum, Mysore Zoo and Chamundi Hills.

description: Dedicated to the presiding deity of Mysore, Goddess Chamundeshwari or Durga (a form of Goddess Sati or Shakti), Chamundi Temple (or Chamundeshwari Temple) is a world-famous Hindu shrine. Located atop Chamundi Hills, where Goddess Chamundeshwari is believed to have killed the demon king Mahishasura, the temple is also a popular Shakti Peetha (among the 51 Shakti Peethas). It is believed that the hair of Goddess Sati fell here and it became one of the 18 Maha Shakti Peethas. The temple’s sanctum sanctorum accommodates a stone image of the goddess and has a small tower at the top. However, the highlight of the temple is its 7-storey high Gopura (or temple gateway tower) which flaunts intricate carvings and is visible from a distance. Another attraction is the huge statue of Nandi Bull (mount of Lord Shiva) made in granite and located alongside the steps leading to the temple. The temple car (chariot) used during festivals is another highlight of the temple. Silver temple doors and Dravidian style construction add to the beauty of the temple. Originally built by the Hoysala Rulers in the 12th century, the temple was improved by the Vijayanagara Empire Rulers and Mysore’s Wodeyar Dynasty Rulers over centuries.

description: The garden city of Mysore is home to many stunning churches, St. Philomena Church being one of them. Considered as the second largest Catholic Church in the entire Asia, it flaunts the spectacular Neo Gothic architecture, borrowing a lot from Cologne Cathedral located in Germany and also from St. Patrick’s Church in New York. It is dedicated to Saint Philomena, who was a teenage Green Princess believed to have martyred in the 4th century. The present day church was built between 1933 and 1936 at the location where an old church of 1843 stood. Apart from the twin spires (each measuring about 175 ft.), it is the floor plan of the church in the shape of a cross which attracts the visitors. With the longer part serving as nave and the arms of the shorter as transepts, the crossing accommodates the altar and the choir. Scenes depicting Christ’s birth, the Last Supper, the Crucifixion, The Resurrection and Ascension of Christ on the stained glass windows add to the church’s grandeur. Daily Holy Mass is performed in three languages – English, Kannada and Tamil. It is interesting to see some of the statues clad in the traditional Indian sarees inside the church. The relic of Saint Philomena, preserved in a catacomb below the altar and the crypt with the saint’s statue are the other highlights.

description: Sri Krishna Temple at Udupi is also known as Sri Krishna Matha and was established by the renowned Saint Sri Madhwacharya in the 13th century. According to the legend, the idol at the temple originally belongs to the Sri Krishna Temple of Dwarka. Once the idol got covered in sandalwood and was being transported by a sailor from Dwarka, when the ship got caught in a storm. Madhwacharya escorted the ship to the shore safely near Udupi and the sailor presented him with the idol as a sign of gratitude. Madhwacharya established the idol at the present day temple, which soon became a popular pilgrimage site. The temple’s administration is looked after by the descendants of Madhwacharya’s 8 disciples, each of whom have established their mathas (monasteries) around the temple. Every 2 years, the festival called Paryaya is celebrated and the administration changes hand from one matha to another. The temple also hosts spectacular celebrations for different Hindu festivals round the year. An interesting aspect of the temple is that the main idol can be viewed from what is known as Kanakana Kindi, a window with nine holes. As per the popular legend, the lord turned towards the window so that his ardent follower Kanakana (who was denied entry to the temple for being non-Brahmin) can see him. Other highlights of the temple include its sacred pond called Madhva Sarovar and temple chariots.

description: An example of how a simple structure can be converted into an architectural marvel, Gol Gumbaz or Gol Gumband (Round Dome) is the mausoleum of Sultan Mohammed Adil Shah. Built in 1656, the monument boasts of a cubical structure with a huge hemi-spherical dome at the top. Its dome and the single inner chamber are counted among the largest of their kinds in the whole world. Each corner of the monument has a 7-storey octagonal tower with a round gallery at the top, offering some great views of the surroundings. The inner periphery of the dome is known as the whispering gallery. Because of its interesting acoustics, the gallery echoes every loud sound nearly 10 times and even the slightest sounds in one part can be heard in the other. Intersecting arches, captivating carvings and mesmerizing scrollwork enhance the monument’s magnificence. Apart from the grave of Adil Shah, it also houses the graves of many of his relatives. Within the complex are some other worthwhile structures like a mosque and a state archaeological museum. A Persian style garden surrounding the structure complements its unbeatable beauty.

description: No wonder that the visitors call it ‘Taj Mahal of the South’! Ibrahim Rauza is capable of casting a spell with its enthralling beauty which makes it hard to leave its premises. The complex consists of the tomb and mosque of Ibrahim Adil Shah II. The two structures look identical and are separated by a water tank and a beautiful fountain. Perforated screens and enticing carvings, along with ornamental entrances and elegant minarets, endow the structures with distinguished magnificence. Tomb’s dome surrounded by rows of carved flower petals is one thing you will love to explore for long. Surrounded by picturesque and lavish lawns that are skilfully manicured, the structures are constantly praised by the authors, poets and history buffs. In fact, many find its grandeur and exclusive design superior to those of Taj Mahal and Humayun’s Tomb. It is also believed that it inspired the Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan for the construction of Taj.

description: Malik e Maiden is called as the Lord of the Battlefield or the monarch of the plains. It is the world’s largest medieval cannon, which weighs 55 tonnes and made a high decibel sound when fired. Malik e Maidan was built by Muhammad Adil Shah-ruler of Bijapur in the 17th century.It is set up in the tallest tower aptly named the Lion Tower because of the majestic lion carvings on the entrance walls. Malik e Maidan is high on the South India’s must see list and despite British attempts to conquer it, it still resides proudly on Indian soil. The cannon is located on the top of the tower and the great Muzzle is shaped like a lion or a dragon with open jaws. Made of cast of alloy of copper, iron and tin, it produced a bell like sound when struck. The polished outside surface of the cannon is dark green and carry inscriptions in Persian and Arabic. The local belief is that touching the outer dark green surface of the cannon made of an alloy of tin, copper and iron brings good luck.

description: Mehtar Mahal is one of the oldest monuments in Bijapur. It is a grand structure built in the Indo Saracenic architecture built by the Adil Shah dynasty in 1620. MehtarMahal is an ornate gateway that leads to a beautiful mosque and a picturesque garden. The mosque is called as the Sweeper’s Palace. As per a local legend, a sweeper from the palace built the mosque within the gateway by using the gold coins spread near the cot ofKing Ibrahim Adil Shah by divine men, after praying for the King’s well-being. Another story attached to MehtarMahal brings forth that the structure is built from the handsome charity received by a Fakir from the king. MehtarMahalis a three-storied structure with beautifully carved arches. It is interesting to note that the carvings are in Hindu architectural style with brackets that support balconies and stone trellis work. MehtarMahalis one of the finest masterpieces of all times and is an architectural wonder. Deep mouldings of the coffered ceiling bring forth astonishing artwork of ancient times. The architecture has various motifs of animals and birds, the most prominent of them being a motif of a lion and an elephant. Many prominent artists like Alexander Nash have travelled across the globe to sketch this beautiful structure. Women are not allowed to enter the mosque.

description: The Alappuzha Lighthouse is India’s first tower to be constructed on the western coast in 1862. It has a great historical significance as it was one of the most popular ports in south India (in older days) and supervised heavy traffic of ships. This beautiful light house is 30 meters high and emits light up to 25 nautical miles. The light house has witnessed all major forms of light sources, from a double wick-oil lamp to a gas flasher, followed by an emergency lantern and finally high voltage Metal Halide light. This magnificent structure of this lighthouse was conceptualized under the guidance of Maratha Varma-II, The King of Travancore and was completed during the reign of King Rama Varma. The lighthouse is a sharp climb up with teak spiral case. Its top provides you with a breath-taking view of the neighbouring countryside converging into the coast line and the majestic setting sun. Adjacent to the lighthouse is a small museum which displays the old lighthouse instruments and is a must watch for tourists interested in antique mechanics. During your visit, other exciting activities which can be explored are house boat rides in the backwaters, visiting archaeological museums, beach side relaxing and snake boat racing. Warning: Steep narrow wooden staircase requires caution for families with small children and people with health issues.

description: Backwaters in Alleppey endow the city with beauty comparable to Venice. The serpent backwater canals are extremely picturesque and tranquil and encourage tourists to enjoy delightful holiday retreats. Traditional houseboats that allow backwater trips and onboard stays arrange for an exclusive holiday experience. Ayurvedic massages and treatments and authentic spicy food of Kerala are offered to the houseboat guests. The backwaters are popular among honeymoon couples, family tourists and nature lovers. Tourists get to explore the palm-fringed banks, exotic birds, churches and monuments and unique rural lifestyle from houseboats. A high level of relaxation can be achieved on the most famous Alleppey Backwaters tours to the destinations like Kumarakom, Kottayam, Mankotta and Alumkadavu. Nearby tourist places include Alleppey Beach and Sreekrishna Swamy Temple. Snake boat races and temple festivals are the most popular events here. Houseboat stay must be booked in advance especially during peak tourist season. Choose backwater packages only from reputed tour operators. Houseboat package prices may vary between INR 7,000 and 15,000 per day.

description: One among the three popular temples dedicated to Lord Krishna in the state of Kerala, the Sri Krishna Temple Ambalapuzha is a revered pilgrimage site. Constructed somewhere around 15th to 17th century, the temple flaunts the heart-warming Kerala architecture and houses the Krishna idol, carved out of black granite and holding a whip and a conch in its arms. Krishna is worshipped here as Parthasarthi which means the charioteer of Arjuna (Partha), one of the five Pandavas from Mahabharata. The major attraction of the temple is the special prasada called payasam (a sweet made of milk and rice) cooked and served to the devotees on everyday basis. The temple is also famous for hosting a number of grand festivals like Ambalapuzha Temple Festival, Aaraattu Festival, Chambakulam Moolam Water Festival, Pallipana and others. One can find beautiful all paintings depicting ten incarnations of Lord Vishnu at the temple. Put on display is Mizhavu, a huge percussion instrument made of copper in the shape of a drum.

description: Presenting a wonderful amalgamation of history and natural setting, Anjengo Fort is a famous landmark in the vicinity of Anjengo – a coastal town located about 12 km. in Varkala. Sandwiched between the sea and backwaters,Anjengo Fortis a place of historic importance. The Anjengo Fort is the place where one can unearth the odds and ends of the old English Fort. Now under the fortification of National Heritage Monuments, tourists visiting this fortcan also explore some notable tombstones and a garden of Anjengo Fort premises. When compared against other forts of Kerala, this fort lacks the distinctive look and feel of an Indian fort. Near the Anjengo Fort, you can take an evening stroll to the nearby Muthalapuzhi Lake. You can also visit some of the nearby attractions viz. Sivagiri Mutt, Janardana Swami Temple, Varkala Tunnel, Sarkara Devi Temple, Kappil Lake, Old Light House and Varkala Beach.

description: Continuously washed by the playful ocean waves, Bekal Fort is the largest fort in Kerala and is known for its strategic defensive construction. Designated as a special tourism spot by the Indian Government, the fort has been the venue for shooting many popular Bollywood movies, songs and music albums. Some of the spellbinding highlights of the fort include stairways eloping into the ground, huge walls and ramparts, water tank accessible by steps, tunnels, observation tower and a magazine that was used to store ammunition. Many amazing structures inside the fort complex include palaces, Hanuman Temple, mosque, Durbar Hall and some outstanding monuments. Bekal Fort offers mesmerizing views of Bekal Beach which spreads in front of the fort. Majestic sunset views can leave anyone speechless. It is indeed a fantasy direct from the fairy tales.

description: Dream World is indeed a dream destination for the fun-lovers and adventure enthusiasts. A modern-day water theme amusement park, it boasts of 42 rides, 24 being the electrifying water rides like Wave Pool, Stormy River, Amazon River, Dream Shower, Dream Splash, Crazy Cruise and many more. In addition, there are 18 equally thrilling dry park rides which include Flying Columbus, Caterpillar, Striking Car, Sky Train, Dragon Train and Revolving Barrel among others. The park boasts of taking special care to offer the best amenities, like high quality filtered pool water and a variety of floats, tubes and rafts. An attraction which is definitely worth mentioning is the Rain Dance section which attracts people from all age groups. Rotating lights and snow showers add to the wonderful visual effects of this attraction. Dream World also boasts of world-class amenities that include video game and multi-game complex, spacious changing rooms, prayer hall, medical facility, ice cream and fast food centres, conference and meeting halls, rest room and free accommodation for students. Students and group visitors are also offered special discounts.

description: Lake Thunakadavu, which has been formed by the Parambikulam Dam, is a scenic water body in the midst of thickly wooded forests of Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary. It is a great place to watch wild life and birds. This lake is popular for spotting the playful Otters that come up to the water surface frequently. The lake is home to numerous crocodiles, which makes it unsafe for swimming. However boating is a good option to explore the lake and its breathtakingly beautiful surroundings. Lake Thunakadavu is deep inside the Parambikulam wild sanctuary and a jeep hire is the best option to reach the lake. Far from the hustle and bustle of the city, the lake is a great place for a relaxed holiday. There is also a watch tower along the banks of the lake for the tourists to have a panoramic view of the forests around. There are tree top cottages for experiencing the beauty of the jungle and the lake at night. There are guided tours where a trained tribal naturalist would accompany the tourists to the forests around to make them understand about the unique flora of the region.

description: Kerala’s biggest archaeological museum, called Hill Palace Museum, came into existence in 1865. Spread over an area of about 54 acres, the museum houses 49 buildings with 14 categories of rare and fascinating exhibits. The most notable is the gallery of royal ornaments and crowns boasting of valuable stones. This particular gallery is accessible only from Friday to Sunday. Other exhibits in the museum include coins, paintings, sculptures, weapons, cutlery, epigraphy samples, inscriptions, manuscripts, chariots, royal furniture and carvings and memorabilia of Kochi’s royal families. More than 200 antiques in the category of ceramics and pottery contribute towards a fabulous assemblage which is just not worth missing. The buildings of the museum are beautifully adorned with wooden carvings. Hill Palace Museum also boasts of a small botanical garden with many exotic species of medicinal plants. A deer park and a pre-historic park are the added attractions. The young visitors can also have great time at the children’s park. Horse riding is a fun-filled activity for the visitors. Cameras and gadgets are not allowed inside the museum, but photography is permitted at various parks in the campus.

description: Guruvayur temple is an ancient Hindu temple in South India which is dedicated to Lord Krishna. This extraordinary place is believed to be holy abode of God Vishnu on Earth and therefore is one of the most important places for worship in South India. The architecture of the temple is incredible with detailed designing at all places. The central icon of Lord Krishna is carrying the Sudarshan Chakra. As per the legends, the deity worshipped here is 5000 years old. The daily rituals of the temple are strictly followed. The head priest is present at all the times to ensure that all the routines and customs are properly followed by all. A proper dress code is adhered by men and women to enter the temple. This beautiful temple is a must visit for all and it is believed to be the lifeline of this town.

description: Renowned for its beautiful flora and fauna worldwide, Eravikulam National Park is domicile to a rare species of the wild goats - Nilgiri Tahr. The park houses about 120 bird species like Nilgiri pipit, black and orange flycatcher etc. Sprawled over 97 sq.km. the national park is famous for the flawless beauty of its roving grasslands and sholas. The presence of Atlas Moth, the largest of its kind in the world, make the park unique. Home to around 100 odd butterflies, the park has several temperate species, medicinal plants and rare orchids. Wildlife enthusiasts can get a glimpse of rare species of fauna like the lion-tailed macaque, leopards, tigers and Nilgiri Langur. The Eravikulam National Park is under consideration for selection as a World Heritage Site. The park remains closed during the calving season from February 1st for 45-60 days. Visitors can enjoy trekking in the Rajamalai Hills. Also, tourists can explore some of the best nearby attractions viz. Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary and Marayoor.

description: Blessed with serene natural beauty, Aralam Wildlife Sanctuary is home to variety of vegetations including tropical evergreen and semi-evergreen, moist deciduous forests and diverse plantations. Wildlife lovers can spot animals like tiger, leopard, porcupine, squirrel, elephant, gaur, deer, civet, macaque and others. The sanctuary is inhabited by numerous endangered bird species including Niligiri wood pigeon, Malabar pied hornbill, Ceylon frogmouth and more. Reptiles, fishes and amphibians can also be spotted here. The sanctuary also offers thrilling trekking adventures to a number of amazing places. Located on the slopes of Western Ghats, it is no less than a paradise for the visitors. Warnings Plastics, polythene, smoking items and liquor are strictly prohibited. Don’t make noise and don’t litter the place. Tourists are advised not to wear white or dark shade clothes.

description: Hundreds of years ago, the historical May 20 of 1498 witnessed the emergence of the first European ship at the Malabar Coast of India. Vasco da Gama, along with nearly 170 Portuguese men, stepped at what is today known as the Kappad Beach. A plaque at the beach testifies the event which would completely alter the history of India. The sea route devised by Vasco escorted many European powers to explore and exploit the riches of Malabar for the next 450 years. A beautiful and calm coastal stretch dotted with coconut groves and flanked by rocky outcrops, the beach is a welcome place for those seeking solitude from urban chaos. Fortunately, the beach continues to remain untouched by crowds and urban developments. After exploring the beach, you can utilize the remaining time to visit an 800-year old Hindu Temple perched on a nearby rock and explore the mesmerizing backwaters of the region.

description: Exquisite settings of luxuriously green hills and valleys at Periyar National Park can overwhelm all your senses. Also a popular Tiger Reserve, the park is spread around Lake Periyar and is extremely admirable for the rich covers of tropical forests, spice plantations, tea and coffee gardens, eucalyptus, orchids and grasslands. Periyar is probably the most amazing wildlife destination of South India, with notable species like Bengal tigers, elephants, gaur, leopard, civets, macaques, jungle cats, Indian wild dogs, Indian giant squirrels and more. The park is also a well-known birding destination with many native and migratory birds regularly spotted here. Trekking, jeep safari, elephant safari and boat safari offer thrilling explorations of the park. Visitors can also enjoy a plenty of eco-tourism activities like bamboo rafting, night trekking, green walks, bullock cart rides and so on. Additional fees are required for each of these activities and also for hiring guides and booking safari trips.

description: Introduction If seclusion and solitude are your ideas for a fulfilling holiday experience, Kizhunna Ezhara Beach of Kannur coastal belt is the ideal location for you. Tow separate beaches of Kizhunna and Ezhara constitute this splendid crescent stretch of golden sand along calm and charming Arabian Sea. Rocks in black colour and crimson-shaded cliffs create an alluring combination with the green waving coconut palms and the visitors get to enjoy deep intimacy with unspoiled nature. While relaxing at the beach is the most heart-warming activity, one can also indulge in bathing and swimming (not recommended during monsoons). Take leisurely walk over the soft sand or sit down to enjoy the warming sun. Book accommodation at one of the homestays offered near the beach and you will relish unparalleled peace and serenity. It can also be a great opportunity to explore the rural lifestyle of the region. Many popular and worthwhile tourist locations near the beach include Kannur Fort (St. Angelo Fort), Arakkal Museum, Thalassery Fort and Aralam Wildlife Sanctuary among others.

description: Kochi is home to a number of old European churches and Basilica of Our Lady of Ransom at Vallarapadam is one of them. An extremely popular Christian pilgrimage centre, the church is dedicated to Mother Mary who is worshipped as Vallarpadathamma in the region. According to the popular legends, Mother Mary protected many ships against the violent storms originating in the Arabian Sea. The beautiful church was constructed by the Portuguese merchants and houses the holy picture of Blessed Virgin Mary. Today it enjoys the status of a national shrine and hosts the grand annual festival in the month of September. Masses are held on weekdays and on Sundays in Malayalam and on Saturdays in English and Tamil.

description: Kochi in Kerala is home to some magnificent colonial marvels, Bolgatty Palace being one of them. Built by the Dutch during their colonial expansion in the region more than 250 years ago, the palace now operates as a heritage hotel and boasts of well-preserved old world charm. Located on the Bolghatty Island, the palace is surrounded by the serene and picturesque backwaters. Staying at the palace hotel is a wonderful experience, as the property offers attractions like swimming pool, ayurvedic wellness centre and a golf course. One can also enjoy the very invigorating Kathakali dance performances here. Other extremely enjoyable activities near the palace include backwater boat cuisse, bird watching, fishing and morning walks. There are deluxe rooms, lakeside cottages, honeymoon suites and other accommodation options to suit different tastes and budgets. Warnings: Booking in advance is recommended if you wish to stay at the heritage hotel.

description: Chinese Fishing Nets is one of the most photographed sites in Kochi because of the breathtaking view. The suspended nets in the mid-air drooping towards the water like hammocks add beauty to this place. These fishing nets were established between 1350 and 1450 AD by a Chinese explorer Zheng He. The most astonishing feature of these nets is that they work on the principle of balance by the bamboo poles. Each of these fishing net is operated carefully by at least four fishermen maintaining their weight on the main plank. This rhythm of balancing a net is spellbinding for the viewers. Fishing is generally done in the morning hours or in the early evening. Sometimes, lights are attached to the bamboo poles and are suspended over the net to attract more fish. This place is especially great for all photo enthusiasts.

description: One of the most popular temples of Kerala, Chottanikkara Rajarajeswari Temple is denoted as Adhiparasakthi Temple. One of the most notable Hindu shrines of Kerala, the temple houses an impressive 5 ft. idol of Rajarajeswari a.k.a. Durga Bhagavathy. Bhagavathy Devi in this temple is believed to have healing power that cures mental disorders. Stationed in a hilly region and embellished with lush green farms, the temple is blessed with utmost serenity and gives mental peace. One of the biggest attractions of the temple is the idol that has four arms and is decorated with jewels, fresh garlands and glittering lights making it appear spectacular. MakomThozhal is an important festival celebrated in thetemple in the month of February and March. Another important festival celebration isNavaratri, which is held during October to November. As the temple is brought from Mookambika Temple in Kollur, it is believed that Mookambika Devi resides in this temple till 7 a.m.

description: Originally constructed by the Portuguese (as a gift to the local rajas), restored and improved by the Dutch and depicting the traditional Nalukettu architecture style of Kerala, Matancherry Palace is a multi-faceted tourist attraction in Kochi. With a quadrangular construction, the palace is very well-known for exhibiting stunning murals. These beautiful paintings portray scenes from various mythological tales of India. Another striking feature of the palace is its floor, which seems to be made of black marble, but has an interesting composition of burned coconut shells, lime, egg whites and charcoal. Apart from the mesmerizing murals, there are collections of royal umbrellas, regal attires, coins and stamps, along with a royal ivory palanquin and many other interesting items. A temple dedicated to Pazhayannur Bhagavati stands in the courtyard, while temples dedicated to Lord Shiva and Lord Krishna flank the palace’s either side. With its quaint charm and fascinating serenity, the palace is a must-visit attraction on Kochi tours. Photography is strictly prohibited inside the palace.

description: The Mattancherry Synagogue popularly called as the ParadesiSynagogue is located in Fort Kochi, Kerala. Built in 1568, it is one of the oldest synagogues and is functioning even today.The central piece of interest in the Mattancherry Synagogue is the Scrolls of the Law written in ancient writingencased in silver and gold. The synagogue has many features that are rare including the Belgium glass chandeliers hanging from the ceiling dating back to the 19th century. The copper plates dating back to the fourth century are written in mirror image writing. The synagogue houses an oriental rug gifted by the last Ethiopian Emperor and even the hand painted floor tiles which boasts of each design being unique from the rest.The floor of the synagogue showcases exquisite hand-painted blue willow patterned floor tiles, imported from China in the 18th century by Ezekiel Rahabi, a renowned Jewish businessman. The speciality of these tiles is that no two tiles are the same.

description: Designated as the oldest European Church in India, St. Francis Church in Kochi was constructed in the beginning of the 16th century. This was the time when the Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama had discovered the Indian coast and paved the way for the likes of Pedro Cabral and Afonso de Albuquerque. Originally built as a wooden church, it was reconstructed as a brick, mortar and tile church from 1506 and 1516 and was dedicated to St. Anthony. Captured by the Dutch in the 17th century, it was handed over to the British in 1804 and St. Francis was declared the new patron saint. The church was the original burial place of Vasco da Gama when he died in 1524. Though, his remains were transferred to Lisbon later, the gravestone marking the original burial place is still intact. Dutch cemetery (one of India’s oldest) can also be found here. Interesting exhibits include the pankhas or fans from the colonial era and the Doop Book (register with records of baptism and marrianges). Today, it is a protected monument, an historical landmark and one of the prime tourist spots in the city. Services are held every Sunday.

description: Cheraman Juma Masjid is very unique as it is the first mosque to be built in Indian subcontinent for Juma prayers. It is a spectacular structure which was inspired from Hindu art and architecture. One of the remarkable factors of this mosque is unlike other mosques face the direction of the Mecca, this one faces the East. This elegant mosque displays the strong bond between Hindus and Muslims in olden days since it follows the Hindu tradition of lightening a brass oil lamp. The oil for the lamp is however, contributed by different visitors on auspicious occasions. This remarkable structure can be visited by people from any religion but only male visitors are allowed to enter the mosque with prior permission. In this thousand years old mosque, lies a marble block which is considered to be sacred since it has been brought from Mecca.

description: The Marthoma Pontifical Shrine is located in the village of Azhikode in Thrissur, Kerala. The church lies on the banks of the River Periyar and is part of the Muziris Heritage Project. The Marthoma Pontifical shrine is built to replicate the St. Peter’s Basilica in Rome and covers over 3500 sq. ft. The significance of this church is that it was the very first church set up the apostle St. Thomas in Kerala and it was followed by six others. The legends surrounding the church speak of how St. Thomas came here in 52 A.D and is believed to have settled in Kerala and later his bones where brought from Italy and where placed in the shrine for worship. The MarthomaSmruthiTharangam Mansion built in the Indo-Persian style depicts the life of St. Thomas. When tracing the roots of Christianity in India, this shrine is said to have been the source of the spreading of the belief. This shrine is one of the major Christian pilgrim centers that are frequented by thousands of devotees worldwide.

description: Sree Kurumba Bhagavathy (or Bhagavati) Temple is located in the holy town of Kodungallur in the Thrissur District of Kerala. Dedicated to Goddess Bhadrakali, a form of Goddess Kali and known in the region as Kodungallur Amma, the temple holds great religious significance and is a popular pilgrimage site. Spread over 10 acres and surrounded by the huge trees of banyan and peepal, the temple has the sanctum sanctorum accommodating a hypnotic image of the goddess with 8 arms, each carrying a different weapon or symbol. According to the popular legend, the temple was established by Lord Parshurama (an incarnation of Lord Vishnu) to honour Bhadrakali who freed the region from the terror of demon Daruka. The temple also houses the images of Lord Shiva, Lord Ganesha and Veerbhadra. Devotees offer red-dyed dhotis (a kind of traditional cloth) and turmeric to the goddess. Pushkarini, the holy pond within the premises, is where the pilgrims take bath before praying at the temple. The temple hosts many festivals and celebrations like Bharani Festival and Thalappoli Festival. The town is also home to many other holy temples, apart from mosques, churches, forts and colonial structures.

description: Located on the northwest slopes of the Western Ghats in Kerala, the Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary, inaugurated in the year 2010 is the 16th Wildlife Sanctuary in Kerala. It falls under the Niligiri Biosphere Reserve and is part of the Wayanad Elephant Reserve. Spread over an area of 75 sq. km, it is home to a rich and diverse flora and fauna. The mountainous terrain of the sanctuary forms the unique and complex mountain systems of Western Ghats that include various mountains like Banasuramala (2050m.), Vellarimala (2100m.), Kurichyarmala (1606m.), Kakkanmala (1456m.) and Vannathimala (1505m.). Malabar Wildlife Sanctuary, which also comprises of the watersheds of various reservoirs also meet the drinking water requirements of the region. The salubrious climatic conditions make it a favourite hill station for tourists especially during the summer months. There are many rare and unique animals and birds in this sanctuary, some of them are endemic to the region. It is home to more than 110 types of birds, 40 types of mammals and different species of butterflies, rare amphibians and reptiles. Some of the highly endangered species found in this region include the lion tailed macaques. This sanctuary is also one of the best places to spot Asiatic elephants in their natural habitat.

description: Kumarakom bird sanctuary is set on the banks of the picturesque Vembanad Lake in Kottayam District in Kerala. Also known as Vembanad Bird Sanctuary, it was developed by an Englishman in a rubber plantation and was originally known as Baker’s Estate. Spread over 14 acres of lush green wooded forest, Vembanad bird sanctuary is well known for the huge flocks of migratory birds from as far as Siberia. Wood peckers, waterfowl, cuckoo, owl, egret, heron, cormorant, moorhen, darter, Brahminy kite the duck night herons, paradise fly catchers, Siberian cranes, king fishers- the list of birds that have made this sanctuary their home is literally awe inspiring. A cruise along the Vembanad Lake and Pathiramanal Island, which is a small island in the lake, is the most delightful option to experience the beauty of this sanctuary and the diversity of the avian population. Nestled on the southern banks of the KavanarRiver, the bird sanctuary has well laid out paths to enable the visitors to explore it on foot. Early morning is the best time to spot the birds when they fly in groups over the lake from their nests.

description: Mahatma Gandhi Beach and Park, which is situated at Kochupilamoodu, is 2 km. away from the Kollam town. It is an important tourist attraction of the Port city of Kollam in South Kerala. The park, which was set up in 1961 is widely popular among families and holiday groups.The unique location of Kollam beach and its proximity to the backwaters makes it a great holiday spotsuitable for a leisurely stroll along the shoreline. There are many popular attractions nearby including the Marine Aquarium, which is the first of its kind in the state of Kerala. The towering 144 ft. tall light house that is 100 years old is another important landmark near the beach. In addition, you can see the remnants of Portuguese and Dutch forts and ancient churches that date back to the 18th century.

description: Malayattoor Church is located in Ernakulam district of Kerala and is one of the most important Christian pilgrim centres in Kerala. Situated atop the 609 m. high Malayatoor Hill, this church is dedicated to St. Thomas. The church that has now been accorded an international pilgrimage station gets visitors from all over the world. Legends surrounding the shrine speaks of how St. Thomas, the apostle of Jesus Christ came here in AD 52. He is believed to have prayed at this spot and drew a cross dedicated to Jesus. He spent his life helping the poor and died here. Many years later the locals found that the cross he drew had turned into a gold cross with his footsteps on the hill. The church is a renowned Christian pilgrim destination and many people come climbing the mountain on foot carrying gold crosses as an offering. During the Lenten season the believers pray for hours and many volunteer groups are set up to provide a feast for them.

description: Located about 18 km. north of Marayoor on the scenic Western Ghats of Kerala, Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary is home to a unique range of plants and animals that are unique to the spiny vegetation. Spread in the area over 90 sq.km., it is designated as the second dwelling for the endangered grizzled giant squirrel in India. Recognized as the wildlife reserve since 1984, the sanctuary is rich in flora and fauna. According to a research, Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary houses about 34 species of mammals, 156 species of butterflies, 52 species of reptiles and 252 species of birds. Renowned as a home to the famous ‘White Bison of Manjampatti,’ the sanctuary houses a unique species like sambar deer, star tortoise, yellow-throated bulbuls and panthers. The Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary is one of the 12 wildlife sanctuaries among the protected areas of Kerala. The sanctuary is nominated by the UNESCO World Heritage Committee for selection as a World Heritage Site. The nearby Thoovanam waterfalls and Sandalwood forest are the best attractions to explore.

description: Famous for its picturesque beauty and perfumed fragrance of tea, Munnar Tea Gardens presents to you a wonderful opportunity to capture the process of picking tea leaves. Visited by thousands of tourists each year, Munnar Tea Gardens is the finest attraction of this hill station. While walking through the mesmerizing tea gardens, visitors can spot Neelakurinji – a flower that blooms every 12 years. One may also encounter elephants, tigers, rare species of Nilgiri Tahr and flying squirrel along with the tea gardens. Tourists can see TATA Tea Company that has a museum at the Nallathani estate. The tea leaves from these gardens are considered as the best souvenir. Early morning is the best time to explore the unadulterated beauty of Munnar Tea Gardens. Visitors can explore the nearby attractions like Thattekad Bird Sanctuary, Mattupetty Lake, Top Station, Eravikulam Park and Kolukkumalai tea estate – believed to be the highest tea garden, with a height of 8000 ft.

description: Counted among the most amazing biodiversity hotspots of the world, Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary is most admired for its spectacular landscapes, heart-warming greenery and treasures of wild animals. Also a popular Tiger Reserve, the sanctuary is spread over 285 sq. km. in the Sungam Hills (between Anamalai and Nelliampathy Hills) of Western Ghats. Thick forests of bamboo, teak, sandalwood, rosewood and neem lend mind-blowing magnificence to this tropical heaven. Parambikulam attracts huge number of tourists for being home to the majestic royal Bengal tigers. Other wild animals inside the sanctuary include leopard, gaur, sloth bear, wild boar sambar, lion-tailed macaque, bonnet macaque, Nilgiri marten and flying squirrel. Some very important reptiles, including king cobra, wolf snake, cane turtle, Western Ghats flying lizard, rock python, forest ground gecko and mugger crocodile, also find home at the sanctuary. More than 260 bird species, nearly 125 butterfly species and many types of fishes can also be spotted here. Added attractions of Parambikulam are Kannimara Teak Tree (world’s largest and oldest living teak specimen), Thuvaiar Waterfall, rivers, valleys, manmade reservoirs and many more. Tourists get to enjoy trekking, boat riding, bamboo rafting and many other exciting activities, apart from savouring the inimitable beauty of this amazing place.

description: Known for its length and unadulterated beauty, Muzhapilangad Beach is perfect and safe for swimmers. Stretching across four kilometres, this beach is the largest drive-in beach in Asia. Outlined by black rocks, it protects the beach from strong currents. The beach is heaven for adventure lovers; visitors can enjoy thrilling activities like parasailing, water bike ride, microlite flights, catamaran ride and power boating and paragliding etc. Young visitors can enjoy car stunts performed on the beach. Through Muzhapilangad, one can also capture the gorgeous view of the Dharmadam Island or the Green Island. Calm waters, glittering sands, swaying palm trees and many eateries selling Malabar cuisines, Muzhapilangad Beach is although less explored, it is definitely worth visiting.

description: With interesting pictorial writings and petroglyphs dating back to Stone Age and Neolithic Age (about 8,000 years old) to their credit, Edakkal Caves promise thrilling expeditions to the initial stage of human evolution. Located on Ambukuthi Mala Mountain in the Western Ghats, these are more appropriately described as fissures with huge boulders roofing the clefts. Discovered at the end of the 19th century, they are believed to have been inhabited a number of times. Many interesting mythological tales and folklores are associated with these one-of-their-kind caves in South India. Apart from all kinds of motifs, shapes and symbols like triangles, stars, squares, tridents, crosses and wheels, the walls of the cleft also showcase human and animal figures, along with the images of a variety of weapons and tools. Some of the pictures are believed to have mysterious resemblance with Indus Valley Civilization. It is an unbelievable experience to explore these iconic open galleries surrounded by lavish greenery of the region. Tourists also get to enjoy the magnificent views of the natural vistas using a telescope installed nearby.

description: An old but one of the best preserved forts in India, Palakkad Fort flaunts historical charm and unique grandeur. Also known as Tipu’s Fort, it was built in the 18th century by the ruler of Mysore Hyder Ali and later fell into the hands of the British. Today, it boasts of strong walls and bastions and stands as a landmark of the region. Among the highlights of the fort is its Kota Maidanam which earlier serves as the royal stable for elephants and horses. The pillared hall within the premises hosts a small museum which exhibits stone carvings and sculptures from the bygone era. An open-air auditorium called Rappadi and a children’s park called Vatika are among other attractions at the fort. One may also visit the Hanuman Temple inside the fort. Well-known for its invincible defence system, the fort is surrounded by water and luxuriant greenery. The town has many other attractions, including temples, heritage buildings, dams, parks and gardens, in its close vicinity.

description: Mata Amritanadamayi Math is an internationally recognized charitable organization. Founded in 1981by Mata Amritanadamayi, the main aim of this organization is spiritual awakening and enlightenment. The organization is highly involved in various causes like removal of poverty, disaster relief, healthcare for the poor, environmental programs and educational facilities and scholarships for students from economically weak segment. Mata Amritanandamayi has millions of followers and believers across the world and is lovingly addressed as Amma. The residents here are dedicated to spend their life in serving God and society. Mata Amritanadamayi Math is located in Amritapuri-a village in Kollam district, Kerala.The Math was awarded the esteemed Dharma Khadgam Award by the President of India for its various charitable involvements. Mata Amritanadamayi Math is an organization that encourages people from all walks of life to participate in its various activities and the students of the Amrita Vishwapeetham schools are often involved in various activities like women empowerment, meditation programs and Sanskrit classes. This is an organization that beckons to all those who want to bring a change in the world by helping others. The headquarters of Mata Amritanadamayi Math is home to more than 3,000 people, comprising of people from all sections including students and monastic disciples. Believers visit the math to receive the blessings of Mata Amritanandamayi and to participate in the bhajans (spiritual songs) and other community programmes.

description: Chimmini wildlife sanctuary was developed in 1984 and is a storehouse of biodiversity. It contains various kinds of evergreen trees, different species of mammals, reptiles, amphibians and fish. There are around 110 varieties of birds and it is not difficult to spot the migratory birds during their season. An exciting tourist attraction inside this sanctuary is the Chimmony dam which is built above the Chimmony River. This place is bliss for all butterfly lovers because of their rich population. Different expedition activities like trekking, bamboo rafting, camping are available for the adventure travellers. However, a prior permission needs to be taken for camping activities. Special trekking path is provided in the sanctuary for trekkers.

description: The forests of Palapilli-Nelliampathy are home to the Peechi-Vazhani Wildlife Sanctuary which covers around 125 sq. km. of the catchments area formed by the Peechi and Vazhani Dams. Wildlife enthusiast as well nature lovers can spend wonderful time at the sanctuary, exploring a huge variety of orchids, medicinal herbs and many species of evergreen, moist deciduous and semi-evergreen trees. Wildlife at the sanctuary comprises of Bengal tiger, leopard, elephant, bison, spotted deer, sambar, bonnet macaque, rusty spotted cat, elk and more. In addition, many species of birds and snakes and other reptiles also find home at the sanctuary. Apart from exploring the sanctuary, you can visit other interesting attractions like Peechi Dam, Vazhani Dam and a number of temples and historical places nearby. Boating is also possible at the dam reservoirs.

description: Established in 1855, Napier Museum is no less than an icon in the city of Thiruvananthapuram. Admirable for its Indo-Saracenic Architecture, featuring gothic roofs, minarets and outstanding ornamentation, the museum building in itself is an attraction worth exploring. The museum houses rare historical and archaeological collections of artefacts, ornaments, ivory and wood carvings, bronze idols, traditional musical instruments, temple chariot, textiles and a variety of handicrafts. The museum complex is also home to the Natural History Museum which exhibits the galleries of mammals and birds, skeleton galleries and fossil tree trunks. Yet another attraction in the premises is Sree Chitra Art Gallery, which has fine collections of oil paintings by Raja Ravi Verma and work of other eminent painters from China, Japan and other countries. Roerich paintings, murals and miniature paintings are other highlights of the venue. Napier Museum shares the premises with Trivandrum Zoo, another must-visit attraction to watch animals like Bengal tiger, Asiatic lion, leopard, Asian elephants, Indian rhinoceros, zebra, giraffe, hippo and many more.

description: Dedicated to Sree Padmanabhaswamy, a form of Lord Vishnu, Padmanabham Temple is among the wealthiest and most popular Hindu temples. The main attraction of the temple is the huge idol of Lord Padmanabhaswamy in the reclined pose over Anantha, the gigantic five-hood serpent. The idol also features a Shiva Lingam, Lord Brahma in Lotus and goddesses Sridevi and Bhudevi. It is believed that more than 12,000 shaligrams (aniconic form of Vishnu) were used to make the idol. The idol is so large that it can be viewed from three doors. The temple complex also accommodates shrines dedicated to other deities like Ramaswamy with Seeta and Lakshman; Narasimha Swamy, Vishwaksenan, Krishna Swamy, Ganapathi, Hanuman and Garuda. Flaunting superlative architecture, the temple boasts of exquisitely carved pillars, nine entrances, a seven-storey and 35 m. high Gopuram, an 80 ft. high golden flagstaff, a corridor with 365 and a one quarter monolithic pillars, magnificent murals, huge number of bells and a temple tank. Many popular festivals are celebrated at the temple. Only Hindus are allowed entry to the temple. The visitors must strictly follow the dress code (dhoti for men and sari for women).

description: Painting a beautiful picture of the luxuriantly green Western Ghats, Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary is rich in diverse vegetations and treasures of wild animals. Dotted with forested hills and fed by the tributaries of River Karamana, the sanctuary offers natural habitat to the likes of tiger, leopard, elephant, sloth bear, wild dog, lion-tailed macaque, Malabar squirrel, Nilgiri langur, barking deer and bonnet macaque among others. The natural splendour of the sanctuary is enhanced by the forests of tropical evergreen, west coast semi-evergreen, southern moist mixed deciduous and hill valley swamp types. It is also a paradise for birdwatchers with frequently spotted species like egrets, kingfishers, darters, cormorants, Malabar hornbills and sunbirds. Butterflies and reptiles like python and cobra can also be explored here. Trekking is one of the best activities to do at Peppara. There are many mesmerizing waterfalls like Kalarkad and Vazahuvanthole that can be easily accessed. You may also like to explore the tribal settlements of the region.

description: Situated about 8 km. from the city, Shankhumukham Beach is one of the famous places to visit in Thiruvananthapuram. Full of interesting attractions, the beach is situated adjacent to the Thiruvananthapuram airport, making it even more popular. The beach has an eye-catching mermaid (MatsyaKanyaka) shaped white statue, which makes visiting the place wonderful. Some of the nearby attractions that make this beach more appealing to the visitors are Chacha Nehru Traffic Training Park, indoor recreational club and thestar fish shaped restaurant. Shankhumukham Beach is also known for its gorgeous sunset and ritual washing of temple idols during local festivals. Tourists to this beach can enjoy other nearby places like Veli Tourist Village, Zoological Park, Vizhinjam, Thiruvananthapuram Observatory, Ponmudi and Agastyakoodam etc.

description: A small yet beautiful village in Kerala, Nattika is renowned for its beach called Nattika Beach. This picture-perfect beach is adorned with glittering sands, clean water, soothing breeze. This beach is also renowned as the shooting destination of Chemmen, a popular Malayalam movie in the 1960’s. Blessed with a wide sandy coast and dotted with swaying palm trees, Nattika Beach attracts many tourists every year. Apart from the spellbinding beauty, this beach is also famous among visitors for its backwater cruise, beach volleyball, angling and many other activities. Unfolded in about 4.5 km. this beach is also popular among bikers. Vehicles can be easily driven through this beach. Numerous chic resorts, restaurants and hotels are located near the Nattika Beach.

description: Kerala’s smallest national park, called Pampadum Shola National Park, has gained recent recognition for its exotic natural beauty and impressive biodiversity. Located at the eastern face of the southern Western Ghats, the park is home to the endangered Nilgiri marten and many other wild species like tigers, elephants, gaur, leopards, Indian wild dogs, wild boar, sambar, barking deer and many more. Many varieties of birds and butterflies add to the park’s exquisiteness. Usually covered with mist and clouds, the park enjoys pleasant weather and offers remarkable views of luxuriantly green grasslands and forests. Apart from many varieties of trees and climbers, there are several herbs with medicinal qualities. Trekking is the best way to explore the amazing treasures of the park. Many eco-tourism activities like adventure trekking and night camping are arranged for the tourists by the combined efforts of forest authorities and local tribes.

monumentName: Vypin Island of City Cochin Cherai Beach
description: Deservingly called the Princess of the Arabian Sea, the miles long splendid Cherai Beach is a paradise for fun-lovers, honeymooners as well as for the family holidaymakers. It is the westernmost beach on Vypin Island and is extremely admirable for its cleanliness and shallowness. Tourists can enjoy relaxing sunbathing experience or a thrilling swimming adventure at the beach. Lavish fringes of coconut trees and luxuriantly green paddy fields add worth to the overall beauty of the beach. Other popular adventures at Cherai Beach include water scooter riding, canoe riding and speed boat riding. It is also possible to spot dolphins which certainly add to the delight of being there. Chinese Fishing Nets, the iconic attraction of Kochi, are also visible from the beach. Walkways illuminated by the tall mast lamps make the beach equally charming at nights. Children can enjoy at a park located near the beach, while the adults can definitely savour the delicious seafood served at various beachside restaurants. December hosts Cherai Beach Tourism Fair, attracting flocks of tourists who get to enjoy a number of cultural art forms.

description: Alchi Gompa (Alchi Monastery/Alchi Choskhor) is an old unique Buddhist monastery complex located in Alchi Village in western part of Leh. Administered by Lirik Monastery, it has its history dating back to more than 1000 years. Built on a flat ground, it is a favourite halt point for several Leh-Ladakh travellers. The complex was created by Richen Zangpo, in the 11th century, who was the Mahaguru and decoder of the Sanskrit manuscripts into Tibetan. The monastery houses several Buddhist temples, chortens, inscriptions and wall paintings. Sum Tsek and Alchi Du Khang are two main temples in the complex. Another celebrated temple, Rinchen Lhakhang, dates back to 1000 AD and covers the largest area. Surrounded by Apricot trees, Alchi monastery is a treat for the eyes of all visitors. The ancient wall paintings and the murals at Alchi showcase the most spiritual and exclusive Tibetan art reflecting the sacred particulars of Buddhists and Hindu kings of that era in Kashmir. The main entrance to the temples is through wooden doors which are marvellously carved and relate to European architecture. During your visit to Alchi Gompa, interesting adventure activities include mountain biking, camping, monastery touring. You can also enjoy the magnificent mountain views or try rafting between Leh and the assembly of Indus and Zanskar Rivers. Warning: It is worth carrying a flashlight due to the lack of electricity. Photography is prohibited here.

description: Qudsia Begum, better known as Gohar Begum, became the first woman ruler of Bhopal at the tender age of 18. A capable ruler and popular among her subjects, she got Gohar Mahal constructed in the year 1820. The palace is an architectural marvel that blends Hindu and Islamic architecture elements and boasts of a charming appearance even in the present day poor state. Occupying the banks of Upper Lake, the palace is on the way to revive its past glory with the ongoing renovation efforts. The three storey structure is known for its fascinating archways, intricate woodwork, paintings and other remarkable features. Apart from being a heritage treasure of Bhopal, the monument is also a popular venue for organizing handicraft fairs and exhibitions. Beautiful sculptures, carvings and a variety of crafts and artefacts are on constant display at the palace round the year. The views of the lakes from palace’s roofs add to its tourism value.

description: One of the most gorgeous architectural wonders of Bhopal, Shaukat Mahal is a unique mix of oriental and Hesperianmanner of architecture. Decorated with floral patterns and soaked in white washed marbles, the palace has a sequent of triangular-mouldedarcs. The impressive Shaukat Mahal is lay by Sardar Manzil (serves as a Public Audience Hall) – a red brick building palisaded by lush green lawns. The exteriors of the palace present a wonderful illustration of eco-friendliness. The royal palace is visited by several visitors every year due to its unadulterated beauty and serene atmosphere surrounding the Mahal. Tourists to Shaukat Mahal can explore nearby attractions like Moti Masjid, Jai Masjid, Shamla Hills, Museum of Man, Bharat Bhavan and Udaygiri Caves.

description: Enclosed in the rustic Vindhyachal ranges, Bhimbetka Caves and rock shelters portray the earliest signs of human race in South Asia. Designated as World Heritage Site by UNESCO, the caves boast of rock paintings which date back to more than 30,000 years and classified into periods like Upper Palaeolithic, Mesolithic, Chalcolithic, Early Historic and Medieval. The place gets its name from Bhima, one of the five Pandavas who spent some time here during their exile period. More than 240 caves have been located in the region out of which only 15 are open for public viewing. Done in colours like red, white, green and yellow obtained from sources like animal fats, plant leaves, manganese, soft red stone, wooden coal and hematite, the paintings and drawings depict themes like dancing, hunting, childbirth, animal riding, animal fights and numerous animal figures. Surrounded by lavish green forests, these rock canvases are a must-visit to explore the earliest days of human evolution. Warnings Don’t scratch or damage the cave walls exhibiting valuable paintings.

It is recommended to hire a taxi for the whole journey. One needs to climb and trek a lot to explore the place fully.""}

description: National Chambal Sanctuary, also known as National Chambal Ghariyal Wildlife Sanctuary, is a river sanctuary well-known as the protected area for magnificent but endangered species of ghariyal crocodile, Ganges river dolphin and red-crowned roof turtle. The sanctuary was formed on River Chambal in 1978 and is spread over the tri-junction of Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh. Other key attractions of the sanctuary include mugger crocodile, smooth-coated otter, three-striped roof turtle, narrow-headed soft shell turtle and so on. Tourists also get to explore a variety of mammals like wolf, striped hyena, Bengal fox, golden jackal, mongoose, wild boar, chinkara, civet, porcupine and others. The sanctuary is also an Important Bird Area, featuring more than 300 species of residential and migratory birds. Boat safari is the most stupendous way of exploring the attractions at the sanctuary. Other activities include camel safari, jeep safari, bird-watching and photography.

description: Gwalior fort is the most popular and the biggest fort in the entire state of Madhya Pradesh. It is believed that this fort has witnessed various eras including the Guptas, the Pathans, the Mughals and the English, therefore is rich in history. All the rulers have left their mark on this structure which makes it more interesting to study. The spectacular Hindu and Islamic architecture of this magnificent monument inspires and mesmerizes many artisans and historians. There are several palaces inside the fort each named after the different rulers who ruled the fort. Some temples inside this monument explain many stories of the past. It offers a breathtaking scenic view of the Gwalior city from the top of a small hill. The sound and light show held in the fort is overwhelming and should not be missed.

description: Also popular as Bhedaghat, Marble Rocks is a gorge, got its shape by the beautiful waterfalls named Dhuandhar. Zooming in gleam ing lustre, the Marble Rock is nestled in the city of Jabalpur unfolded in about 3 km. in length. Dubbed as one of the finest holiday destinations of India, Marble Rocks appears splendid during the dark when the moonlight broadcasts on the rocks. Rising about 100 feet high above the flowing river, the soft and shining marbles are wrapped up with dark green and black volcanic creases making it awesome in appearance. The tranquil environment, holy water of Narmada and magical effects at the night make Marble Rocks truly a paradise on the earth. The beauty of the Marble Rocks can be best explored while taking a boat ride in Narmada River. For the spectacular view and thrilling experience, tourist prefers a cable car ride that takes you through the gorge. The area has many other tourist attractions like Tripur Sundari Devi, Chousat Yogini Temple, Bargi Dam, Dhuandhar waterfall and many others.

description: Lal Baag Palace (or Lal Bagh Palace) is an impressive three storied structure that stands on the banks of River Khan. Famed as one of the spectacular city edifices in the city, the palace was built by Maharaja Shivaji Rao Holkar during 1886-1921. Once considered as the pride of Holkar Dynasty, Lal Baag Palace remains as the symbol of the glorious past of the city. Lal Bagh palace exudes beauty and regal charm in its design. The ornate gates of this palace are exact replica of the gates of Buckingham Palace and were casted in London. The opulence and grandeur of the palace is well augmented by its many beautiful gardens and spectacular architecture. The main attraction of the palace is its elegantly furnished rooms with beautiful carvings on the walls and ceilings. The palace has now been converted into a museum that is open for the common public who can take a peek to the life style of the rulers of the leading dynasty of Indore. It has a collection of many interesting artefacts and historical items.

description: Visitors to the Kanha National Park instantly realize why the protected forest reserve inspired Rudyard Kipling for his magnum opus ‘The Jungle Book’. Spread over 940 sq. km. area, the park was created in 1955 and soon it became a part of the Kanha Tiger Reserve along with other nearby forest reserves. While royal Bengal Tiger is the most spellbinding attraction of Kanha, one can also enjoy exploring the likes of leopards, slothe bear, swamp deer (barasingha), spotted deer, sambar, bison, nilgai, hyena, jungle cat and wild lives of other sorts. Kanha is also famous as a bird watching destination for being home to storks, egrets, pintails, herons, rollers, drongo, kingfishers, finches, green pigeon, woodpeckers and many more. Apart from over 1,000 flowering plants, the park also boasts of sal trees, bamboo forests, huge meadows and the beautiful Indian ghost tree called kullu. Kanha is internationally claimed for its star tigers like Munna which regularly feature in wildlife documentaries. Safari trips to the park are organized twice a day, from sunrise to 11:00 a.m. and from 02:00 p.m. to sunset. Guides (that cost extra) are compulsory for the visitors. Limited numbers of vehicles (18-40) are allowed per shift to the four zones of the park.

description: Enjoying the designation of the highest point of Satpura Range and also of the state of Madhya Pradesh, Dhoopgarh (or Dhupgarh) is one thing that every tourist to Pachmarhi is recommended. Well-known for its sunrise and sunset views, it is a vantage point with unbound natural splendour spread all over. Visitors to the place enjoy the 360 degree view featuring dense forests, rivers, ravines and valleys. At the height of about 4,429 ft. from the sea level, Dhoopgarh is also known by the name of Harvatsa Kot. Fortunate visitors can enjoy the peak glory of the place when engulfed in thin monsoon fog. It is literally the marvellous experience of standing amidst clouds. Don’t forget to carry a camera to take some memorable shots from the place. A small museum at Dhoopgarh is the added attraction for the visitors.

description: India’s one-of-its kind assemblage of fossils can be explored at Ghughua Fossil National Park, located close to the city of Jabalpur. The specimen discovered at the park have added to the strong belief that there existed Gondwanaland, one of the two super-continents of which India was a part along with Australia, Africa and many other parts. Fossils put on display at the park date back to the period about 65 million years old. Interestingly, the fossils found in the park have their living relatives that can be located in the regions as far as Africa. For example, a eucalyptus fossil of the park has its living counterpart in Australia. Likewise, there are fossils of plants like jamun, palm, aonla, jackfruit and many more. In addition, one can explore the fossils of leaves, fruits, seeds, flowers and climbers at this national park. Because of its invaluable possessions, the place earned the designation of a national park in 1983. Tourists can visit the interpretation centre of the park to obtain detailed information about the fossils.

description: Even the prestigious acclaims and designations, including UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the Seven Wonders of India, are inadequate to praise the magnificence of Khajuraho Group of Temples. Built by the artists with unparalleled skills under the supervision of Chandela Rulers between 950 and 1150, the temples portray the magnificent art of sculpturing in the most exclusive manner. Divided into Western, Eastern and Southern groups, there are nearly 25 temples (though, more than 80 existed originally) dedicated to the Hindu and Jain deities. The world is familiar with Khajuraho Temples as those depicting the erotic art of lovemaking through sculptures. Couples posing in different Kamasutra postures are indeed the best highlight of these timeless structures. Made of sandstones, the temples boast of exquisite carvings, friezes and sculptures depicting potters, farmers, nymphs, dancers, musicians and people engaged in different categories. Some of the most popular of these temples are Kandariya Mahadeo, Devi Jagdamba, Chaunsath Yogini, Parsvanath, Dulhadev and Chatturbhuj. Standing amidst lavish green lawns, each of these structures is a manmade wonder. The Western Group of Temples hosts two light and sound shows (first in English and second in Hindi) every evening, telling the glorious tales of the place. The ticket costs INR 350 for foreigners (INR 200 for children) and INR 120 for Indians (INR 60 for children). The temples also host the annual Khajuraho Dance Festival in February which is a must-attend event.

description: Dedicated to Lord Shiva, the Omkareshwar Temple near Coorg is well-known for its unusual architecture and amazing peace in its surroundings. The temple was constructed in 1820 by the local king Lingarajendra II to get rid of the sin of killing a Brahmin. Unlike the usual Hindu Temples, it flaunts the blend of Kerala, Islamic and Gothic architectural styles. Red-tiled roofs and golden dome surrounded by four minarets add to the temple’s elegance. Bars made of panchaloha and featuring alphabetic lims are the notable feature of the temple. The main shrine at the temple accommodates a Shiva Lingam which originally belongs to the holy city of Kashi (Varanasi). A beautiful tank with a narrow path leading to a mandapa is an added attraction. It is a firm belief of the devotees that praying at the temple wholeheartedly can fulfil all the wishes.

description: The huge Orchha Fort Complex houses the most glittering gem of Bundela Rajput architecture, the Jahangir Mahal. Named after Mughal Emperor Jahangir, the palace was built in 1598 by the local ruler Vir Singh Deo who was a dear friend of the emperor. One can savour the exclusive blend of Rajput and Islamic architectural elements that result into this truly marvellous structure. Alluring hanging balconies, bulbous domes, huge porches and courtyards and over 100 rooms with eye-pleasing decorations bless the structure with soul-touching grandeur. Massive gateways are exquisitely embellished and the front walls are prettified with turquoise tiles. Two gigantic stone elephants with hanging bells welcome the visitors to this outstanding royal spectacle. Overlooking River Betwa, the palace and other attractions within the fort complex make you feel out of this world. The palace is also known as Rani Mahal as it once served as the residence of King Madhukar Singh’s wife. Her room boasts of beautiful paintings depicting scenes form Hindu mythology. A small museum nearby is also worth visiting. The entry ticket is valid to explore the palace as well as other splendid monuments of the fort.

description: Established as a national park in 1981 and declared a tiger reserve in 1994, Panna National Park is an award-winning wildlife reserve in the Central India. Mainly popular for royal Bengal tigers, the park is home to many other wild species like leopard, sloth bear, hyena, wild dog, caracal, sambar, chital, chinkara and nilgai. A number of reptiles and birds can also be spotted at the park. Fed by River Ken, the park has a core area of about 543 sq. km. and hosts diverse topography in the form of hills, plateaus, valleys and gorges. It is strategically located at the point which separates the South India’s tropical and subtropical dry forests from the moist deciduous forests of Gangetic Plains. Pandav Fall and Raneh Fall are the must-visit attractions at Panna. Safari trips are possible in private vehicles (jeeps and mini buses) and on elephants. Additional fees must be paid for visiting the falls and also for the guides.

description: Bandhavgarh National Park was founded in 1968 and it soon became a wildlife hotspot, mainly because of high tiger density and great chances of spotting the royal beast. Apart from tigers, the park is home to many other wild species including gaur, sloth bear, leopard, bison, striped hyena and many more. The park is no less than a paradise for bird-watchers because of the presence of species like egrets, teals, vultures, eagles, jungle fowls, peafowl, peacock and kingfisher. Jeeps are available for thrilling safari trips and once can also opt for the equally fascinating elephant safari trips. Many other attractions in and around the national park include Baghel Museum, Bandhavgarh Fort, Cheshpur Waterfall and some old temples. Warnings Don’t litter the park and maintain silence.

description: Once the hunting preserve of the Maharajas of Gwalior, Madhav National Park is one of the ancient protected areas of the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. Established as Madhya Bharat National Park in 1955, Madhav National Park got its current name in the year 1959. Spread over an area of 355 sq.km. Madhav National Park is renowned for its monuments related with the Gwalior State as well as its flora and fauna. The park comprises of dry deciduous mixed and dry thorn forest. One can see some of the prominent trees in this national park like khair (acacia catechu), salai, dhawda, tendu and palash etc. Home to migratory birds like pochard, geese, pintail, mallard and teal, Madhav National Park showcases one of the finest attractions – the George Castle, built by JivajiRaoScindia. One can watch the beautiful sunset and gorgeous surroundings from the top of the park. The park has about 227 species of birds. Animal lovers can enjoy the sight of wild animals like deer, nilgai, sambar, blackbuck, leopard and chinkara. Visitors to this national park can also explore nearby attractions like SakhyaSagar Lake, Sailing Club, Madhav Lake, George Castle JalMandir and Siddha Baba.

description: Mahakaleshwar is one of the twelve Jyotirlingams and among the \ most famous Shiva temples in the country. Located in the ancient city of Ujjain in the state of Madhya Pradesh, this beautiful temple stands on the banks of the Rudra Sagar Lake. Shiva in the lingam form is believed to be Swayambhu, which originated all by itself unlike other lingams that are ritually established. The idol of Mahakaleshwar, which faces the south is known to be dakshinamurti. The temple is built in five levels one of which is under ground. Located in a sprawling courtyard surrounded by imposing walls, the temple is famous for its intricate carvings and designs. On the day of Maha Shivaratri, a huge fair is held near the temple, and worship goes on through the night

description: Known as India’s most important tiger breeding habitats, Melghat Tiger Reserve houses more than 35 tigers at one place. Unfolded in about 1676 sq.km. it is one of the first nine tiger reserves under the Project Tiger mission. Stationed near Madhya Pradesh-Maharashtra border, Melghat Tiger Reserve houses two interesting attractions – the Melghat Sanctuary and the Gugarnal National Park. Other than tigers, Melghat Tiger Reserve is also popular as a fantastic birdwatching spot. It has about 250 bird species and 16 snake species. Renowned as the region of cliffs and mountains, tourists may have to wait for a long time to spot tigers as they are not easily seen. Visitors can spot raptors and eagles in large number here. Leopards, wolves, foxes, jackals, hyenas and Dholes are some of the other animal species tourists can find here. Other nearby attractions includesAmbadevi Temple, Chikhaldara Wildlife Sanctuary, Gugamal National Park and Sridham Temple etc.

description: Envoy of mesmerizing Indian wall painting that flourished between 2 BC and 7 AD, Ajanta Caves consist of 30 rock-cut cave monuments. Dedicated to Buddhism and Jataka Tales, the murals on the walls are extremely spellbinding and so are the sculptures and architectural elements of these caves. Carved along a horseshoe-shaped cliff along River Waghora, the caves boast of mystic ambiance and lush green surroundings. A UNESCO World Heritage Site, these caves have been designated as the protected monuments by the Archaeological Survey of India. While few caves represent the Hinayana phase of Buddhism, while others are believed to belong to the Mahayana phase. Monasteries, stupas, sanctuaries, viharas, chaitya-grihas and verandas are the main sections. Few of the caves continue to be incomplete. Murals at Ajanta consist of decorative patterns and are done using a variety of shades. Some equally majestic caves can be found at Ellora at the distance of about 105 km. Warnings: Carrying a torch can be handy as it may get dark inside the caves.
